ddls:
CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "ssn" TEXT /* Social security number */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender (m/f) */,
  "birthdate" TEXT /* Birthdate */,
  "maiden_name" TEXT /* Maiden name (if applicable) */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address */,
  "city" TEXT /* City */,
  "state" TEXT /* State */,
  "zip" INTEGER /* Zip code */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of customer location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of customer location */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role or permission level */,
  "preferred_communication_method" TEXT /* Preferred communication method */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "payment_methods" (
  "payment_method_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each payment method */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who owns the payment method */,
  "cc_type" TEXT /* Type of credit card (e.g., Visa, Mastercard) */,
  "ccn" TEXT /* Credit card number */,
  "cc_cvc" INTEGER /* Credit card verification code */,
  "cc_expire_date" TEXT /* Credit card expiration date */,
  "billing_address" TEXT /* Billing address for the payment method */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the payment method (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "credit_limit" REAL /* Credit limit for the payment method */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("payment_method_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_payment_methods_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "transactions" (
  "transaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each transaction */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who made the transaction */,
  "payment_method_id" INTEGER /* ID of the payment method used */,
  "transaction_date" TEXT /* Date of the transaction */,
  "amount" REAL /* Amount of the transaction */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the transaction (e.g., success, failed) */,
  "transaction_type" TEXT /* Type of transaction (e.g., payment, refund, chargeback) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description or notes for the transaction */,
  "processing_fee" REAL /* Payment processing fee for the transaction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("transaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_transactions_payment_method_id FOREIGN KEY ("payment_method_id") REFERENCES payment_methods ("payment_method_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_transactions_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_roles" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role assigned to the customer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id", "role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "transaction_id" INTEGER /* ID of the transaction associated with the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., payment failure, credit limit reached) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_transaction_id FOREIGN KEY ("transaction_id") REFERENCES transactions ("transaction_id")
)
column_names: Unique identifier for each payment method
